evals.name: post-merge-ci-sync
evals.prompt: I just merged a big PR into main — re-sync the Browzer workspace so both the code graph and the indexed docs reflect the new state. This is running in CI so make sure output is machine-readable.
evals.expected_output: Runs `browzer workspace sync --json` (or `--save <file>`). Does NOT run `browzer init` or `browzer workspace docs --add`. Optionally prefaces with `browzer status --json` for the pre-flight probe.

assertions.text: Calls `browzer workspace sync` (or `browzer sync`) as the primary command
Does NOT call `browzer init` (workspace already exists)
Does NOT call `browzer workspace docs --add` (sync never adds new docs)

evals.name: docs-only-resync
evals.prompt: Nothing about the code structure changed, I just edited a few markdown files under docs/. Can you push the updated docs to Browzer without re-parsing all the code?
evals.expected_output: Runs `browzer workspace sync --skip-code` so only the document reconcile step executes. Explains that `--skip-docs` would be the opposite and that `workspace index` alone would NOT re-upload docs.

assertions.text: Does NOT run `browzer workspace index` on its own (that's code-only)
Explains that new-never-indexed docs still need `embed-documents`

evals.name: dry-run-before-destructive
evals.prompt: I deleted a bunch of old docs locally as part of cleanup. Before I let Browzer actually delete them from the workspace, show me exactly what the sync would do.
evals.expected_output: Runs `browzer workspace sync --dry-run --json --save <path>` (or equivalent), reads the plan, and surfaces the `docs.deleted` list to the user for confirmation. Does NOT pass `--force` or run the mutation yet.

assertions.text: Uses `--json` or `--save` to capture the plan machine-readably
Waits for user confirmation before running the non-dry sync
